title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Contract Lens: Startup Readiness Liveness
summary: The service reads configuration during requests, starts background promises without ownership, mixes module systems, treats signals as ordinary exceptions, and reports ready before dependencies exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts startup readiness liveness increment in process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Sequence configuration, dependency creation, listener binding, liveness, readiness, background ownership, and atomic startup failure.
Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Startup Readiness Liveness · Theory Startup Readiness Liveness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2626 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
